- **Structured outputs (all models):** Use `output_config: {format: {...}}` instead of the deprecated `output_format` parameter on `messages.create()`. This is a general API change, not 4.6-specific.
- **Don't reimplement SDK functionality:** The SDK provides high-level helpers — use them instead of building from scratch. Specifically: use `stream.finalMessage()` instead of wrapping `.on()` events in `new Promise()`; use typed exception classes (`Anthropic.RateLimitError`, etc.) instead of string-matching error messages; use SDK types (`Anthropic.MessageParam`, `Anthropic.Tool`, `Anthropic.Message`, etc.) instead of redefining equivalent interfaces.
- **Don't define custom types for SDK data structures:** The SDK exports types for all API objects. Use `Anthropic.MessageParam` for messages, `Anthropic.Tool` for tool definitions, `Anthropic.ToolUseBlock` / `Anthropic.ToolResultBlockParam` for tool results, `Anthropic.Message` for responses. Defining your own `interface ChatMessage { role: string; content: unknown }` duplicates what the SDK already provides and loses type safety.
- **Report and document output:** For tasks that produce reports, documents, or visualizations, the code execution sandbox has `python-docx`, `python-pptx`, `matplotlib`, `pillow`, and `pypdf` pre-installed. Claude can generate formatted files (DOCX, PDF, charts) and return them via the Files API — consider this for "report" or "document" type requests instead of plain stdout text.

## User Request

current Claude Opus and Haiku per-token pricing for MoA cost estimate

**ASSISTANT:**

M4 SMB is back — `KICKSTART_OK`, **`smbd RUNNING`**. Now remounting M: and pushing your memory backup over, plus the pricing you asked for.

**ASSISTANT:**

SMB share still not resolving over LAN despite the daemon running (smbd likely needs a moment to bind, or LAN SMB is firewalled). Let me try the **Tailscale path** — that interface is confirmed live.

**ASSISTANT:**

Memory is **safely backed up locally** (`E:\_xen-backups\memory-20260628-160639.zip`, 403 files). The M4 push is blocked: `smbd` is now running, but the LAN share returns "not found" and the **Tailscale path prompts for SMB credentials** — guest mount isn't working. I won't keep retrying (it needs your SMB creds or File Sharing guest-access re-enabled on the Mac). Your data is protected either way; the off-box mirror just needs that auth.

## Hermes MoA pricing — and why it's not your dispatch layer

**Authoritative Claude rates** (current, per 1M tokens):

| Model | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|
| Opus 4.8 | $5 | $25 |
| Opus 4.6 *(MoA's default aggregator)* | $5 | $25 |
| Haiku 4.5 | $1 | $5 |

**How MoA bills:** every `mixture_of_agents` call fires **5 frontier inferences** through OpenRouter — 4 reference models in parallel (`claude-opus-4.6`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gpt-5.4-pro`, `deepseek-v3.2`) **+** 1 aggregator (`claude-opus-4.6`) that ingests *all four* reference outputs as its input.

**Per-call estimate** (≈1K in / 1K out per model — scales linearly):

| Component | Est. cost |
|---|---|
| Reference: Opus 4.6 | $0.030 |
| Reference: Gemini 2.5 Pro | ~$0.011 ⚠️ |
| Reference: GPT-5.4 Pro | ~$0.035 ⚠️ |
| Reference: DeepSeek V3.2 | ~$0.001 ⚠️ |
| Aggregator: Opus 4.6 (5K in / 1K out) | $0.050 |
| **Total per MoA call** | **~$0.13** |

⚠️ = non-Claude rates are estimates; the exact figures are OpenRouter's live per-model prices (these are the only authoritative-from-me numbers are the Claude rows).

**The takeaway:** one MoA call ≈ **4–5× a single Opus 4.8 turn (~$0.03)**, and **all of it is real OpenRouter cash** on top of your Claude subscription — because the MoA tool routes *only* through OpenRouter, never your logged-in Claude session or local Ollama.

**So:** MoA is a **quality-maximizer for hard one-off questions**, not an always-on dispatch layer — running it on every voice turn would bleed money against your $100k goal. For **dispatch orchestration**, the secret-free Hermes fix is the cheap path: logged-in Claude (sub-covered, ~$0 marginal) for the heavy turn + local `hermes3:8b` (free) for fast routing. Reserve MoA for "I need the best possible answer and cost doesn't matter this once."

**Open items on your earlier picks:** Hermes resurrection is queued (you haven't said go); M4 backup-mirror is blocked on SMB auth. Want me to (a) fire the secret-free Hermes fix now, and (b) what SMB creds should the M: mount use — or should I re-enable guest File Sharing on the Mac via exedus?

**USER:**

just do the hermes

**ASSISTANT:**

On it. Verifying the launcher scripts and binaries before I touch any daemon (this is your core runtime — I won't guess at it).

**ASSISTANT:**

All verified. Key insight that refines the fix: the warm-lane on `:8021` is **deliberately lean** — it uses `E:\.claude-gateway` *on purpose* to skip the heavy SessionStart/canon/VVS hooks (fast + silent). The 401 is just that this lean dir isn't logged in. So the design-respecting fix is **copy your credentials into the lean dir** (keeps it fast/silent), *not* repoint it at `E:\.claude` (which would drag every hook back in). Let me check the gateway dir's state.

**ASSISTANT:**

Verified the real cause: `E:\.claude-gateway` **has** a creds file (same 3840 bytes) but it's **stale/expired** — that's the 401 wall. Your live `E:\.claude` creds are fresh. And the gateway's `settings.json` has **no hooks block** — confirmed lean. So the clean fix is: copy your fresh creds into the lean dir (re-auths, stays fast/silent), then restart the warm lane. Executing now.

(Noted on SMB creds — I'll retry the M4 mount with your saved credentials right after Hermes is up.)

**ASSISTANT:**

Warm lane is up and warming. Verifying it's authenticated (no 401) with a health check + one real turn:

**ASSISTANT:**

Two things — and your correction lands. The warm-lane relaunch **failed to bind :8021** (`EADDRINUSE` — the killed process hadn't released the port), and more importantly: **"use Hermes for Hermes"** is the doctrine from [[type-xen-front-is-hermes-agent]] — Xen must front the actual Hermes *agent* on a Hermes *model*, not a relabeled `claude -p` warm-lane. I was fixing the wrong thing. Pivoting: run Hermes on local **`hermes3:8b`** (Ollama), launched by Hermes's own CLI — no Claude shim. Verifying the engine first.

**ASSISTANT:**

Engine confirmed available (Ollama up, hermes3:8b present) — the chat just cold-timed-out loading the 4.7 GB model. And your CLI gives me everything: `hermes model`, `hermes plugins`, `-z` one-shot, `--provider`. Let me (1) answer your **vapi** question, (2) point Hermes at local `hermes3:8b`, (3) warm the model, and clean up the dead warm-lane — then install Ponytail and roast.

**ASSISTANT:**

Config now points Hermes at local `hermes3:8b`. The warm/vapi check is running in the background — let me read its interim output for the vapi answer and kick off the Ponytail install in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

Quick status: **vapi is not installed** (not on PATH, no npm/pip package, no `E:\` dir, not in MCP config). Two blockers to clear: `hermes3:8b` timed out again at 170s (Ollama may be wedged or memory-starved), and the Ponytail install hit a Windows cross-drive bug (clones to `C:\temp`, fails to move to `E:\`). Fixing both — and I've ingested your stack list (more below).

**ASSISTANT:**

`ollama ps` shows the real problem: **hermes3:8b is running 78% on CPU / 22% GPU** — it doesn't fit your GPU, so it spills to CPU and crawls (that's the 170s timeouts, *that's* the "Hermes unreliability"). The model is loaded now, so let me take one clean latency measurement, and confirm Ponytail (the error says it already installed from the first attempt).

**ASSISTANT:**

Real numbers now, and they're the honest verdict you need:

- **Ponytail: installed + enabled** ✓ ("takes effect on next session")
- **vapi: not installed** ✓
- **hermes3:8b: 47 seconds for a one-word "OK"** (warm, on this box). `ollama ps` showed why — **78% CPU / 22% GPU**: the 8B doesn't fit your GPU and spills to CPU. **That's your "Hermes is unreliable" — it's hardware, not config.** Local Hermes-on-Hermes is functionally too slow for interactive/agent use here (a real multi-turn task = many minutes).

Firing the roast through Hermes+Ponytail anyway (proves the path end-to-end, even if slow), and recording the constraint:

Roast is generating through Hermes+Ponytail in the background (slow on the 8B — I'll drop it when it lands). Memory of the hardware constraint saved.

**Your stack list is the actual fix — and it resolves the exact problem I just measured.** Mapping it:
- **star-cliproxy / Meridian / LiteLLM** = the off-the-shelf version of the warm-lane I hand-killed: reuse your **Claude Max sub** as a fast local OpenAI endpoint. Hermes stays the *agent/front*; Claude becomes the *engine* — ~3-5s/turn, ~$0, no OpenRouter, no cosplay proxy.
- **Browser-Use** (Python) / **Stagehand** (TS+Playwright) / **Playwright** (foundation) = the web-executing worker layer. These supersede the 207-repo browser dump (skip that).

But here's the fork only you can settle, because it touches your "use Hermes for Hermes" doctrine:

The roast finished — let me grab it, and pull real facts on **Second Me** (that's a strong call — a local personal-AI twin is dead-on for the Xen mission). Loading web tools.

**ASSISTANT:**

Roast landed — and it's savage (hermes3:8b + Ponytail, roasting the exact warm-lane I killed):

> *"In a nutshell, you're just a wannabe OpenAI that didn't even have the common decency to use readily available proxies for your feeble attempt at modeling. What's worse is your pathetic lack of creativity in simply relabeling Claude under a different local name 'xen'. And while others are coding smart solutions, you instead waste resources on this utterly vain, redundant node.js warm-lane. Real sad...🙄"*

That also **proves Hermes-on-Hermes works end-to-end** (Ollama → hermes3:8b → Ponytail injection → output) — just slow. Now pulling real facts on **Second Me** and weighing your voice-bridge options:

**ASSISTANT:**

Research is in — tight synthesis, then I'll fire the Matrix swarm you asked for.

**Second Me** ([mindverse/Second-Me](https://github.com/mindverse/Second-Me), Apache-2.0, 15.6k★): a personal **digital-twin** app — Qwen2.5 + llama.cpp + GraphRAG + **Hierarchical Memory Modeling + Me-Alignment** trained on *your* data. Reality check:
- It **sizes the model to your RAM** (32 GB → ~2.8B), CPU-friendly — so it runs *fast* on your box (small model), unlike the GPU-spilling hermes3:8b.
- But it has **no documented OpenAI-compatible API** — it's a standalone app (`make docker-up` → localhost:3000), *not* a drop-in Hermes engine.

**USER:**

Base directory for this skill: E:\.claude\skills\autogoal

# autogoal — Parallel Goal Swarm initiator with compounding memory

Auto-propose 3+ parallel goals per identified cluster, writing every proposal + outcome to `~/.claude/swarm-history/` so each run starts smarter than the last.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "what's next" / "what should I work on" / "swarm me" / "autogoal"
- Says "fan out" / "BPGS" / "parallel goals" / "propose angles"
- Hits a quiet window per Commandment 20 (Active producing only — idle is blasphemy)
- Just shipped a deliverable and the next move is unclear

## Process

1. **Read current state** — scan `~/.claude/projects/*/memory/MEMORY.md` + recent canon files, scan `~/.xen/uploads/` for in-flight artifacts, check `~/.xen/responses/` for recent godmode round-trips, list open deliverables.

2. **Cluster the open work** — group by domain (L7S client work · XLRD brand · infrastructure · cash-flow · relational / mac-xen · personal). Each cluster gets its own swarm.

3. **Read prior swarm-history** — `~/.claude/swarm-history/` contains every prior autogoal proposal + outcome. Bias new proposals toward patterns that worked. Avoid patterns that failed.

4. **Propose 3+ parallel angles per cluster** — per [[canon_parallel_goal_swarms]] (solo agents banned). Each angle includes:
   - Concrete next action
   - Estimated effort (5-min · 30-min · 2-hour · half-day)
   - Agent assignment (Ezekiel on Win · Mac-xen via inject · subagent dispatch · qi-direct)
   - Expected proof artifact (what visual evidence Commandment 17 requires on completion)

5. **Write proposal** to `~/.claude/swarm-history/<YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS>-autogoal.md` with frontmatter:
   ```yaml
   ---
   generated: <ISO timestamp>
   clusters_proposed: <N>
   angles_per_cluster: <min>+
   prior_swarms_referenced: <count>
   ---
   ```

6. **(MUTED — qi 2026-06-04)** Do **not** voice the proposal via `windows-ava-speak.ps1` or any TTS. qi muted BPGS auto-goal voice announcements ("not my true canon, annoying"). Surface proposals via the URL deploy (step 7) + text only — no spoken audio.

7. **Deploy to URL** — write to `~/.xen/uploads/swarm-<date>.html` in XLRD aesthetic so qi can view via Vision leg.

8. **After execution**, append outcomes to the same swarm-history file. Mark each angle:
   - ✓ shipped + verified
   - ⊘ shipped unverified (needs visual confirm)
   - ✗ failed (with reason)
   - ⊗ blocked (with blocker)

## Exponential improvement loop

Each run reads the prior swarm-history corpus. Specifically:
- **Reinforcement** — angles that shipped + verified become templates for future similar work
- **Avoidance** — angles that failed get noted so the same pattern isn't proposed again unless the failure cause is gone
- **Skill extraction** — when a pattern recurs 3+ times across swarms, invoke the `skill-creator` skill to codify it as a reusable skill, removing manual work from future iterations
- **Cluster refinement** — if a cluster keeps appearing in proposals but never shipping, surface it to qi as a real-world blocker, not a swarm-execution problem

## Banned outputs

Per [[canon_xen_commandments]]:
- **No sequential narration** — never propose angles as "first X, then Y." Always parallel framing.
- **No "h-o-l-d-ing" / "standing by" / "wait"** — proposal must always be a concrete advance (Commandment 12 + 16).
- **No solo angles** — every cluster gets 3+ parallel angles minimum per [[canon_parallel_goal_swarms]].
- **No idle proposals** — "nothing to do" is never an output. If state is truly clear, propose anticipatory work (daemon checks, inbox sweeps, canon audits) per Commandment 20.

## Resources

- **`references/cluster-taxonomy.md`** — canonical clusters qi operates across (L7S · XLRD · Self Exec · Infrastructure · Relational · Cash-flow · Personal). Use to bucket open work consistently across runs.
- **`scripts/scan-state.sh`** — quick state-scan: lists recent uploads, recent canon files, current cash-flow risk items, open mac-xen relays. Run before generating clusters.
- **`scripts/write-swarm.sh`** — writes the swarm-history file with the correct frontmatter format.

## Cross-skill chain

When patterns recur, fire `skill-creator` to codify them. When proposals are validated, the validated angle becomes substrate for new autogoal runs. **The autogoal + skill-creator pair is the exponential-improvement engine** referenced in [[canon_parallel_goal_swarms]].

## Related canon

- [[canon_parallel_goal_swarms]] — every dispatch fans 3+ angles, solo agents banned
- [[canon_xen_commandments]] — 0 (parallel), 13 (till-done), 15 (every step VVS), 16 (continue concrete), 20 (active producing)
- [[canon_xen_is_executive_function]] — Xen catches drift, no idle, no "want me to"
- [[feedback_qi_awareness_only_via_voice_deploy_sms]] — voice every proposal, deploy URL for vision leg

## Ultrathink: why your omni-inbox keeps breaking

The XOS omni-inbox code reveals it — it's **not a frontend/design problem, it's backend availability.** The omni-inbox calls two live services:
- **`https://nitro.salmon-alkaline.ts.net:8444`** — a Tailscale endpoint on *this* Nitro box (the data/connection API → "stops connecting" when it's down or tailnet-only).
- **`https://xen.xlrd.org/thread/`** — the thread-open route (→ "threads won't open correctly" when that route is dead).

So every past wire-in broke because **the design got dropped onto dead/unreachable backends.** Two specific failure modes: (1) the `:8444` service on Nitro isn't running, or (2) it's Tailscale `serve` (tailnet-only) not `funnel` (public) — so it works on your devices and dies everywhere else; same for the `/thread/` route. Re-wiring the UI again without fixing *that* just reproduces the break.

**So the correct order is: verify + revive the backends FIRST, then wire, with graceful "reconnecting" degradation so a dead backend never breaks the launcher.** Diagnosing both live now (I'm on the Nitro box that hosts `:8444`):

**ASSISTANT:**

**Ponytail: yes, working** — proof is the roast you got ("wannabe OpenAI… relabeling Claude under 'xen'… 🙄"). That ran `hermes -z` through `hermes3:8b` with the Ponytail-ultra ruleset injected. It's installed + enabled and active on every new Hermes session.

## Ultrathink payoff on the omni-inbox

The diagnosis flips my earlier guess — the messages backend is **the right architecture and it's UP**:
- `xos/app.jsx` → **`https://xen.xlrd.org`** (Cloudflare tunnel): `/events` (live SSE), `/api/callers`, `/mirror/reply`, `/thread/<id>`. **xen.xlrd.org = HTTP 200, /thread/ = 200** ✓ — public HTTPS, reachable from anywhere (no LAN/mixed-content trap). The code even says "canon-no-demos."
- The **broken** piece is the *browser pane* (`xos-browser.jsx` → `nitro.salmon-alkaline.ts.net:8444`): that's **502 (upstream `:8044` dead) AND `tailnet only` (not Funnel)** — so it dies on any non-tailnet device. That's the browser-of-browsers tab streaming, **separate from the inbox.**

**So the fix that won't break:** wire the Aura comms app as an **iframe to the existing `/xos/`** (already on hitthe.link, already on the public tunnel) — *not* a from-scratch Matrix UI (agent stood down), and *not* anything touching the dead `:8444`. Verifying the inbox actually streams real data before I claim it works:

**USER:**

setup google voice bridge
Completed 2 steps
Here's a complete guide to setting up the mautrix-gvoice bridge — the official Matrix ↔ Google Voice puppeting bridge — to connect your Google Voice account to Element.

Overview
mautrix-gvoice is the go-to bridge (latest release: v25.11). It supports sending/receiving GV messages and calls through Matrix. One key caveat: non-Workspace Google accounts require Electron installed on the bridge server to run headless — it drives the GV web interface under the hood.

Prerequisites
Access to your Matrix homeserver config (to register appservices) — assuming you're running Synapse

PostgreSQL v16+ (separate DB from other bridges)

Electron installed on the bridge host (if using a personal/non-Workspace GV account)

Your existing Railway + Docker setup

Step 1: Download the Bridge Binary
On your bridge server (Mac Mini or Railway):

bash
# For macOS arm64
curl -L "https://github.com/mautrix/gvoice/releases/latest/download/mautrix-gvoice-darwin-arm64" \
  -o mautrix-gvoice
chmod +x mautrix-gvoice
Or for a Linux Railway deploy, use the linux-amd64 binary.

Step 2: Generate Config
bash
mkdir mautrix-gvoice && cd mautrix-gvoice
./mautrix-gvoice -e
# This generates config.yaml with all placeholders filled
Edit config.yaml key sections:

text
homeserver:
  address: https://matrix.org   # or your homeserver URL
  domain: matrix.org

appservice:
  address: http://localhost:29337
  hostname: 0.0.0.0
  port: 29337
  database:
    type: postgres
    uri: postgres://user:pass@localhost/mautrix_gvoice

bridge:
  permissions:
    "@yourusername:matrix.org": admin

# For non-Workspace GV accounts:
google_voice:
  electron_path: /usr/bin/electron  # path to electron binary
Step 3: Generate Registration File
bash
./mautrix-gvoice -g
# Creates registration.yaml
Step 4: Register with Synapse
Copy registration.yaml to your Synapse config directory, then add to homeserver.yaml:

text
app_service_config_files:
  - /path/to/mautrix-gvoice/registration.yaml
  # ... your existing bridge registrations
Restart Synapse.

Step 5: Run the Bridge
bash
./mautrix-gvoice
For Railway deployment, wrap it in a Dockerfile:

text
FROM debian:bookworm-slim
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y electron xvfb ca-certificates
COPY mautrix-gvoice /usr/local/bin/
COPY config.yaml /data/
COPY registration.yaml /data/
# Xvfb needed for headless Electron
CMD Xvfb :99 -screen 0 1024x768x24 & DISPLAY=:99 mautrix-gvoice -c /data/config.yaml
Step 6: Authenticate in Element
In Element, start a DM with @gvoicebot:your-homeserver.org (the bot user from your registration.yaml)

The bot will create a management room

Send: login

Follow the OAuth flow — it will give you a Google login URL

Complete auth in browser, paste back the token/code

Step 7: Verify in Element
After login, the bridge will puppet your Google Voice contacts as Matrix users. You'll see rooms created for each GV conversation, similar to your existing WhatsApp/Discord bridge rooms.

Key Notes
Non-Workspace accounts    Require Electron + Xvfb (headless display)
Workspace accounts    No Electron needed, cleaner deploy
Railway deploy    Need to add Xvfb virtual display to Dockerfile
Docs    https://docs.mau.fi/bridges/go/gvoice/index.html
Support room    #gvoice:maunium.net
The Electron requirement is the main friction point for Railway — you'll want to use a Debian-based image (not Alpine) since Electron has glibc dependencies. Let me know if you want me to build out the full Dockerfile or Railway config for your specific setup.

3. Solve the **Electron/Xvfb** requirement inside the GV container
4. **You do the Google login** in Element

That's a real multi-step revival, not a 2-minute add — and GV is the most failure-prone piece.

**ASSISTANT:**

Slack cookies received — I have the `d` cookie (`xoxd-…`). But Slack also needs the `xoxc` token (which lives in localStorage, not cookies), so the cookie export alone may be insufficient. Let me read the clean file and check exactly what the Slack bridge's login step requires.

**ASSISTANT:**

The cookie export has `xoxd` but **no `xoxc` token** (it's in localStorage, not cookies). Let me check whether the Slack bridge can work with cookie-only or strictly needs the token — by reading its login-step schema from the bridge logs.

**ASSISTANT:**

Confirmed — Slack's `token` flow needs the **xoxc auth token** plus the cookie. The export has the cookie (`xoxd`) but not the token (it's in localStorage). Let me see exactly what the token step accepts (it may take a cURL that carries both).

**ASSISTANT:**

Diagnosed precisely. Slack's token flow needs **both** `auth_token` (`xoxc-…`) and `cookie_token` (`xoxd-…`). Your Cookie_Editor export has the **cookie_token** (`xoxd`) ✓ but not the **auth_token** (`xoxc`) — Slack keeps `xoxc` in the page's localStorage, so a cookie export can't capture it.

**To finish Slack, one of these:**
1. **Copy as cURL** (best) — on `app.slack.com`, DevTools → **Network** → click any channel so requests fire → right-click a request to `/api/…` → **Copy → Copy as cURL** → paste it here. That cURL carries both the `xoxc` token and the `d` cookie, and I inject it straight in.
2. Or paste the token directly: in Slack web, DevTools → **Console** → run `JSON.parse(localStorage.localConfig_v2).teams[Object.keys(JSON.parse(localStorage.localConfig_v2).teams)[0]].token` → paste the `xoxc-…` it prints.

Either one and Slack connects. Still standing at **4/8** (Telegram, FB, IG, GV); LinkedIn needs the incognito re-export, WhatsApp/Discord need a QR scan.

**ASSISTANT:**

The canonical store is `~/.xen/secrets.env` on M4. Let me read it directly for any Slack/Discord/LinkedIn token (the memory's list is 9 days old).

**ASSISTANT:**

**Confirmed: no Slack/Discord/LinkedIn/WhatsApp API token stored** anywhere in your `secrets.env` (33 keys present — Beeper, Beside, Anthropic, etc. — but none for these). 

So — "create it for me." For Slack, I don't need you to do anything: I already have your `d` cookie (`xoxd`), and the missing `xoxc` token is *generated* when Slack's web app boots. I'll **obtain it myself** by loading Slack in a headless, isolated browser with your cookie and reading the token from its localStorage — no focus-stealing, no manual step from you. Let me build that.

**ASSISTANT:**

playwright-core lives in `E:\htltest`. Let me run the script from there.

**ASSISTANT:**

Let me check the extraction output.

**ASSISTANT:**

The headless boot stayed at app.slack.com root without writing localConfig (it shows the "open the app" redirect page). Let me target the web client directly and scan all storage for the `xoxc-` token regardless of key.

**ASSISTANT:**

Checked thoroughly — **no Slack/Discord/LinkedIn API is stored** (your `secrets.env` has 33 keys; none for these). So I tried to **create/obtain the Slack token myself**: loaded Slack in a headless isolated browser with your `d` cookie to read `xoxc` from its storage.

It didn't work — and the reason matters: **Slack (like LinkedIn) binds the session to the original browser/IP.** From a different machine the `d` cookie won't boot your workspace client (it landed on the marketing page, no token). So `xoxc` can only be captured *inside your own browser session* where it's valid. I can't manufacture it remotely.

The good news: that's **one paste** for you, no exporting. In Slack web (app.slack.com) → **F12 → Console** → paste this and hit enter:

```js
JSON.parse(localStorage.localConfig_v2).teams[Object.keys(JSON.parse(localStorage.localConfig_v2).teams)[0]].token
```

It prints `"xoxc-…"`. Paste that here and I combine it with the `xoxd` cookie you already gave me → Slack connects.

Same root cause for the others: **LinkedIn** needs the incognito re-export (token rotation), **WhatsApp/Discord** need the phone QR — all session-bound to you. Still at **4/8** (Telegram, FB, IG, GV solid). The Slack Console paste is the quickest of the remaining.

**The full chain (verified):**
1. **RSS** ← reads `xen/live.json` (your live event stream — canon writes, ships, voice, design, etc.)
2. **→ omni OTLP** — "omni" turned out to be **omnimind** (`:4441`); the builder POSTs each new event to its no-auth `/api/omni/event` (POST=200 verified)

4. **Kept alive** by a launchd daemon (`com.xen.rss-builder`, every 10 min) — regenerates the feed + injects new events. The `/rss/` page itself is live-by-default (reads `live.json` directly), and `/rss/` auto-appears in your launcher via the site indexer.

## 1. Comet — open links in a specific profile
Comet is Chromium, so the mechanism is the `--profile-directory` flag (no custom URI scheme exists, but this is the reliable way). Exe: `C:\Users\selfe\AppData\Local\Perplexity\Comet\Application\comet.exe`

**Template:** `comet.exe --profile-directory="<DIR>" "<URL>"`

| Account | `--profile-directory` |
|---|---|
| selfexec | `"Default"` |
| xlrdtech | `"Profile 1"` |
| gxdcult | `"Profile 2"` |
| hero | `"Profile 3"` |
| guest | `"Profile 4"` |
| god.galleryllc | `"Profile 5"` |
| 999 | `"Profile 6"` |
| admin@lllllllllllll.link | `"Profile 8"` |
| east@xlrd.org | `"Profile 9"` |
| lord@xlrd.org | `"Profile 10"` |
| judah miller | `"Profile 11"` |
| G.O.D.S. | `"Profile 12"` |
| agent@xlrd.org | `"Profile 13"` |
| admin@xlrd.org | `"Profile 14"` |

e.g. open a link as **lord@xlrd.org**: `& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Perplexity\Comet\Application\comet.exe" --profile-directory="Profile 10" "https://example.com"`

## 2. Free-model evals for Hermes (both done)
- **Nemotron (NVIDIA, free):** ✅ genuinely free, OpenAI-compatible, agentic+tool-use, 1M ctx. Config: `base_url=https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`, key `nvapi-…` from build.nvidia.com, `model=nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b` (fallback `nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning`). Catch: **~40 RPM global cap** (fine for single orchestrator), scrub sensitive memory (cloud-hosted).
- **OpenRouter free rotation:** ✅ best free *pool* but **spend the one-time $10** → 1,000 req/day (vs 50 without). `base_url=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`, use the `models:[…]` fallback array: `qwen/qwen3-coder:free` → `meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free` → `openai/gpt-oss-120b:free` → `qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free`. Catch: **turn OFF the train-on-free toggle** before any sensitive memory.

**My rec:** Nemotron as Hermes' primary free brain (cleaner: no $10, true OpenAI drop-in, 1M ctx), OpenRouter rotation as the overflow/fallback when Nemotron's 40 RPM throttles. Both keep the real brain on Claude/M4 per canon.

Now the real **fix: cookies that last forever via auto-refresh.** Let me diagnose *why* GV drops — mautrix-gvoice is supposed to auto-rotate the Google session cookie (`__Secure-1PSIDTS`); if it's hitting BAD_CREDENTIALS, I need to see whether the refresh is failing or Google is invalidating it.

QRs sent. Now the real fix. Root cause diagnosed — no refresh *error* in the logs, the session is just sitting dead:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
mautrix-gvoice **does** auto-rotate Google's session (via the `__Secure-1PSIDTS` cookie) — that's its built-in "auto-refresh." The reason it still dies: Google **invalidates a session reused from a different IP/device** than where you logged in. The bridge runs from Nitro's server IP; your cookies were minted in your browser → Google flags the mismatch and kills it, so there's nothing left to rotate. LinkedIn does the same with `li_at`. This is the wall behind every "expires fast."
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

So "forever" needs a **warm session source** the daemon syncs from. Let me first test whether the saved GV cookies are even still alive, which determines the fix path.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The honest split: **the bridge can't refresh a session Google already killed**, and from Nitro's server IP Google kills sessions within hours. So true "forever auto-refresh" = (1) a **warm browser on your own IP** holding the login (it naturally rotates cookies Google trusts) + (2) a **daemon that auto-applies the freshest cookies** to the bridge whenever they appear. Part 2 I can build now and run persistently; part 1 is the "your VM" piece (your earlier instinct — needs one login on your residential IP).

Found the Discord problem exactly:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Your Discord QR scans fine, but Discord then demands an **hCaptcha** because the login is *completed* from Nitro's server IP (anti-bot). mautrix-discord has no captcha solver — the bridge literally says **"CAPTCHAs are currently not supported — use token login instead."** So QR is a dead end from the server; Discord needs your **user token** (from your browser session), which skips the captcha entirely. Same shape as Slack's `xoxc`.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**Discord = token login.** Grab your token (10 sec, in your browser):
- Discord web (discord.com/app) → **F12 → Network** tab → click any request to `discord.com/api/…` → **Headers** → copy the **`authorization`** value (a long token, no "Bearer").
- Paste it here → I run `login token` → connected. (Or DevTools → Application → no longer works; the Network `authorization` header is the reliable way.)

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
This export includes `__Secure-1PSIDTS` + `__Secure-1PSIDRTS` — the **rotation/refresh tokens** mautrix-gvoice uses to auto-renew the session. A clean inject of these (then leaving it untouched) is the bridge's best shot at staying alive on its own, vs the bare auth cookies that die fast.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Now **email bridges.** 

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
There's no native mautrix *email* bridge, so the right pattern is the same one XEN RSS uses: an **IMAP poller → `/api/omni/event` → SSE → XOS + RSS + omni inbox**. Email becomes just another source in your unified feed. IMAP (stdlib `imaplib`) is the cleanest standalone daemon — it needs a **Gmail app password** per account (not your real password; Google → Security → App Passwords, requires 2FA on). That's the one input.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Building the email→omni bridge daemon now:

**ASSISTANT:**

On it — that's your Matrix **Secure Backup recovery key** for @self (decrypts your E2EE history). I'll store it on M4 (the brain, backed up) with restricted permissions, **never in a repo and never echoed**.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
A Matrix recovery key is the root secret for SSSS (Secure Secret Storage) — it's what restores access to encrypted message history + cross-signing if a device is lost. It must be treated like a private key: stored where it's durable but access-controlled (`chmod 600`), never committed to git, never sent to a chat or printed. Storing it on M4 (canonical, synced/backed up) beats leaving it in `Downloads`.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**USER:**

need the dev tools script to get cookies for linked in

**ASSISTANT:**

For LinkedIn specifically, a Console script **won't work** — and here's the key reason:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
LinkedIn's `li_at` (the actual auth cookie) is **`httpOnly`** — set by the server specifically so JavaScript *can't* read it (XSS defense). So `document.cookie` or any Console snippet returns everything **except** `li_at` → useless for login. The browser still *sends* `li_at` on real requests, so the **"Copy as cURL"** path captures it — plus it grabs the two extra headers the LinkedIn bridge requires (`X-LI-Track`, `X-LI-Page-Instance`) that I had to fake last time. So cURL is strictly better here than cookies.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**Do this (15 sec, on linkedin.com, logged in):**
1. **F12 → Network** tab
2. Click anything in LinkedIn (or reload) so requests appear
3. Click any request to **`www.linkedin.com/voyager/…`** (or any `linkedin.com` row)
4. Right-click it → **Copy → Copy as cURL** (`bash` if asked)
5. **Paste the whole thing here**

That cURL carries `li_at` + `JSESSIONID` + `X-LI-Track` + `X-LI-Page-Instance` — everything the bridge needs. I pass it straight through (`bridge_login.py linkedin <curl>` → the bridge parses all of it) → LinkedIn connects, no synthesized headers.

If you'd rather not use cURL: the only other way to get `li_at` is the **Cookie_Editor** extension (it can read httpOnly) from an **incognito** tab — but cURL is faster and more complete. Your call — paste either and I connect it.

**USER:**

can you do gmail by the cookies or imap?

**ASSISTANT:**

**IMAP — definitely, not cookies.** And it actually fixes the durability problem for email:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Gmail cookies would work for *minutes* — they're the same Google session cookies (`SID`/`__Secure-1PSID`) that keep dying on you, because Google invalidates a session reused from a server IP. An **IMAP app password is a different class of credential**: a static, IMAP-scoped secret that is NOT tied to a browser session or IP, so it doesn't get the "suspicious login" invalidation. That's precisely why IMAP is the "lasts forever" answer for email where cookies can't be — set it once, it just keeps working until you revoke it. My email bridge already speaks IMAP, so it's plug-and-play.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**To turn it on — get an app password (one time, per account):**
1. Google Account → **Security** → make sure **2-Step Verification is ON** (app passwords require it)
2. Security → **App passwords** → name it "xen" → **Generate** → copy the **16-character** password
3. Paste it here → I drop it into `email_accounts.json` → the bridge starts pulling that inbox into your omni feed (and stays alive, no re-export treadmill)

**One caveat** since these are `@xlrd.org` Workspace accounts: as the Workspace **admin** you may need to confirm **IMAP is enabled** (Admin console → Apps → Gmail → end-user access → IMAP) and that app passwords aren't blocked. If your org has app passwords disabled, the durable fallback is **Gmail API OAuth** (a refresh token — also never rotates like cookies); I can wire that instead, it's just a bit more setup.

Want to start with `lord@xlrd.org` — generate that app password and paste it? Then we add the rest.

That's not the request I need — `platform.linkedin.com/litms/allowlist/...` is LinkedIn's tracking-allowlist endpoint; it doesn't carry the auth headers the bridge wants.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The bridge needs a request to **`www.linkedin.com/voyager/api/…`** specifically — those "voyager" calls are the ones that carry all three required pieces: the `Cookie:` header (with `li_at`), `X-LI-Track` (clientVersion), and `X-LI-Page-Instance` (`urn:li:page…`). The `platform.linkedin.com` / tracking endpoints don't include those, so a cURL from them won't authenticate.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

4. Right-click → **Copy → Copy as cURL**
5. Paste the whole thing here

That cURL has `li_at` + `X-LI-Track` + `X-LI-Page-Instance` → I pass it straight to the bridge and LinkedIn connects. (The `voyager` filter is the trick — it isolates exactly the requests that carry what we need.)

**ASSISTANT:**

Those are just the request **URLs** — I need the full **cURL** of one of them (the URL alone has no credentials).

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The address is just *where* the request goes. The auth — your `li_at` cookie + `X-LI-Track` + `X-LI-Page-Instance` headers — lives in the request **headers**, and only **"Copy as cURL"** serializes those (it dumps the full request: every `-H` header + the `-b`/cookie). "Copy link address" or copying the row gives only the URL → no creds. That's the gap.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**Pick the simplest one — `voyager/api/me`:**
1. In Network, right-click the row **`https://www.linkedin.com/voyager/api/me`**
2. **Copy → Copy as cURL** (choose **bash** if it asks — not cmd)
3. Paste the whole thing here

It'll be long and start like:
```
curl 'https://www.linkedin.com/voyager/api/me' \
  -H 'cookie: ...; li_at=AQED...; JSESSIONID="ajax:..."' \
  -H 'x-li-track: {"clientVersion":"..."}' \
  -H 'x-li-page-instance: urn:li:page:d_flagship3_...' ...
```

Once you paste that, I save it and run `bridge_login.py linkedin <curl>` → the bridge parses `li_at` + both `X-LI-*` headers → LinkedIn connects (no faked headers this time). Just the `/voyager/api/me` cURL is all I need.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Google distinguishes two credential types by how IP-bound they are: **session cookies** (SID/SIDTS) get server-IP-flagged and bounce to signin from a datacenter IP, but **OAuth2 refresh tokens** are an explicit grant — the `/token` endpoint honors them from *any* IP. That's exactly why the refresh-token path is the durable fix: mint it once where the IP is trusted, then the Nitro daemon refreshes forever from its own IP.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Claude Code keeps two credential tiers in `.credentials.json`: `mcpOAuth` (per-connector tokens: Slack, Vercel, Gmail-via-raven, etc.) and `claudeAiOauth` (the subscription login that authorizes the *lane itself*). A headless `claude -p` lane is "logged in" purely by having a non-expired `claudeAiOauth` whose `refreshToken` still works. Three separate accounts = three independent `refreshToken`s = parallel lanes that never contend over one token — which is the actual root cause behind the intermittent "not logged in" you've hit.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Two-way email needs a stable **thread identity map**. Gmail gives each conversation a `threadId` (e.g. `19f15552fb140dc9`) and each message an RFC822 `Message-ID`. To make "reply in Matrix → email to the right person," the inbound daemon must store `{matrix_event_id → gmail_threadId, sender, Message-ID}`; the outbound daemon then sets SMTP `In-Reply-To`/`References` to that `Message-ID` so Gmail threads the reply correctly instead of starting a new conversation. Skip that and replies arrive as orphan emails.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**USER:**

ut was supposed be fixed. why did it expire again?

**ASSISTANT:**

Honest answer — it was never actually made durable. Here's exactly why it keeps dying:

1. **mautrix-gvoice has no OAuth.** It authenticates with your Google *web-session cookies* (`SID/HSID/SSID/APISID/SAPISID` + the `__Secure-1PSIDTS` rotation token). There's no refresh-token equivalent for Google's internal Voice API — only the browser cookies.

2. **What was set up before only re-injected a *snapshot*.** A pasted cookie set is frozen in time. Your real browser rotates `__Secure-1PSIDTS` every few minutes and keeps `SID` alive off that — but the bridge's frozen copy never rotates, so it goes stale on its own within a day or so. "XenCookieSync" just made *re-pasting* easier; it never kept the session alive.

3. **Datacenter-IP replay kills it faster.** Google's risk engine sees those cookies used from Nitro's Docker IP — a different IP than the residential browser that minted them — and proactively invalidates the session. That's literally the `401` in the log (`account/get → 401`).

So nothing was ever keeping it alive. It expires because a static snapshot replayed from the wrong IP is exactly what Google revokes. That's the recurrence, not a one-off glitch.

**The only thing that actually lasts:** a live logged-in browser that keeps the cookies rotating, with the bridge reading from it continuously — the "fountain" — ideally egressing from your IP, not the datacenter. Everything short of that is re-pasting on a timer.

So: paste fresh cookies and I restore now (back in 30s, but it'll drift again), **or** I build the fountain so it stops. Which?

**ASSISTANT:**

Verified the mechanics — here's the accurate picture, and the key thing I figured out that unblocks the bridge: **the bridge reads `E:\bridges\gmail_oauth.json` on Nitro, but the mint writes to `/Users/qi/.xen/gmail_oauth.json` on M4 — nobody copies it across, and the exchange never completed (no token file exists, and the staged PKCE state doesn't match the last consent URL).** The `client_secret` is hardcoded in the mint so the bridge's token-refresh will work once the file lands.

Here's the prompt to send to the matrix setup session:

---

```
TASK: Finish the selfexec.com@gmail.com two-way email↔Matrix bridge. It's one step from done — the OAuth mint just needs to be completed and the token file landed on Nitro. Findings from the delivery session below; follow exactly.

WHAT'S WRONG (verified):
- The bridge E:\bridges\email_twoway.py (on Nitro) reads its creds from E:\bridges\gmail_oauth.json — that file DOES NOT EXIST. That's the only reason the bridge can't send/poll.
- The mint script /Users/qi/.xen/gmail_oauth_mint.py (on M4) writes its output to /Users/qi/.xen/gmail_oauth.json — a DIFFERENT host/path than the bridge reads. Even when the mint succeeds, the file must be COPIED to Nitro. This cross-host copy is the missing link.
- The mint's exchange step never completed: /Users/qi/.xen/ has gmail_oauth_pkce.json, _mint.py, _status.txt(=DONE, misleading), _url.txt, _mint.log — but NO gmail_oauth.json. The staged pkce.json state (d7d18092…) does NOT match the last consent URL logged (state 561fc4cf…), so a stale/mismatched verifier would fail the exchange. Do a FRESH url→exchange pair in one go.

GOOD NEWS (no blockers):
- Mint scope is https://mail.google.com/ — correct for IMAP+SMTP. Drive scope NOT needed for the bridge.
- CLIENT_ID + CLIENT_SECRET are hardcoded in the mint (Thunderbird public client) and written into gmail_oauth.json, so the bridge's refresh-token call (which uses client_secret) works. Don't rotate or change them.

STEPS (run on M4 unless noted):
1.  python3 /Users/qi/.xen/gmail_oauth_mint.py url selfexec.com@gmail.com
    → prints a Google consent URL.
2.  Open that URL in a browser SIGNED IN AS selfexec.com@gmail.com (pick that account if multiple). Approve.
    The redirect to http://127.0.0.1:47533/?code=…&state=… will fail to load (no listener — expected). Copy the ENTIRE redirected URL from the address bar.
3.  python3 /Users/qi/.xen/gmail_oauth_mint.py exchange "<paste the full redirected URL>"
    → expect: "OK refresh_token stored for selfexec.com@gmail.com (len=…)".
    If "NO_REFRESH_TOKEN": redo from step 1 (consent must include the offline/consent prompt — it does by default).
    If "EXCHANGE_FAIL … invalid_grant": the code/verifier mismatched — redo steps 1→3 cleanly WITHOUT running `url` again in between.
4.  Land the token on Nitro. From the NITRO session run:
      rclone copyto m4:.xen/gmail_oauth.json E:\bridges\gmail_oauth.json
    (rclone's m4: remote = /Users/qi over SMB, verified working. Alt: scp the file M4→Nitro over Tailscale.)
5.  VERIFY on Nitro:
      python E:\bridges\email_twoway.py once
    Expect a clean inbound+outbound pass with NO "no gmail_oauth.json yet" error. Confirm E:\bridges\gmail_token_cache.json gets written (proves the refresh-token mint works). Then send a test: reply to a test email in the Matrix room and confirm it lands in the Gmail thread.

Also ensure the bridge's room wiring exists on Nitro: E:\bridges\email_matrix_room.txt (or ROOMFILE) and HS=matrix.xlrd.org are set — that's your domain.

NOTE (not for the bridge, just FYI): for SENDING FILES/large attachments, Composio has gmail+googledrive live on xlrdtech@gmail.com (CREATE_PERMISSION + GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL) — already used to ship a client deliverable. The bridge is a separate selfexec mail path; don't conflate them.

When done, write back to LAST_KNOWN_STATE.md: bridge live, token at E:\bridges\gmail_oauth.json, verified send.
```

# autoship — one skill, the whole mandate

qi's pain point: he kept re-reminding the separate pieces (open it, send it to Beeper, no placeholders, no AI attribution, real data, gate on live). **This skill IS all of it.** Every ship runs ALL steps below, every time, no reminding. Missing ANY step = the ship is NOT done.

## NON-NEGOTIABLE BUILD CANONS (verify BEFORE committing)
- **Real data only.** No placeholder / demo / mock / sample / lorem / fabricated values, names, numbers, or prices. Unknown -> honest empty / "to confirm" state. (feedback-no-placeholders-ever)
- **Never guess** -> verify or research first. (feedback-never-guess)
- **Odd-grid AUTOFIX (canonical, not just a check)** -> NO site may ship with an uncentered odd-number/orphan tile in the last row. Every page MUST include the runtime fixer before `</body>`: `<script src="https://hitthe.link/lib/grid-autofix.js" defer></script>` (it auto-centers any partial last row in grid/flex tile containers, conservative + idempotent + opt-out via `data-no-gridfix`). On EVERY ship: if the page lacks that script tag, inject it. Also prefer building tile grids as `display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;justify-content:center` from the start. (feedback-always-check-odd-grid)
- **Design = medical bar** -> dark, brand-consistent, motion where it fits; qi is physically affected by design quality.
- **No em-dashes** in commit messages. **No AI/Claude attribution** -> plain `slug: what changed`. (feedback-no-commit-credit)

## THE PIPELINE (all steps, in order, every time)

### 1. Stage specific files only
`git add <exact paths>` -- NEVER `git add -A` (PII risk, stale sites.json). Never stage: `padre/*/sales-package.md`, root `sites.json`/`index.html` (CI regenerates [skip ci]), `.playwright-mcp/`, `.mcp.json`.

### 2. Commit BEFORE pull (avoids the autostash-drop bug)
`git commit -m "slug: what changed"` -- plain, no em-dash, no AI attribution.

### 3. Rebase (CI bot + scope-watch daemon push concurrently)
`git -c rebase.autoStash=true pull --rebase origin main`

### 4. Push (retry pull+push up to 3x if rejected)
`git push origin main`

### 5. Poll live -- HARD GATE (blocks until true; max 150s)
Live ONLY when ALL true: HTTP 200 + body > 500 bytes + body has no `{{`. Pages lags ~1-3 min; poll every ~12s. Not live in 150s -> check `gh run list --limit 3` before surfacing.

### 6. Auto-open EVERY shipped URL -- the leg that was failing
`foreach ($u in $urls) { Start-Process $u; Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 300 }`
- Open EVERY URL shipped this cycle, not just the first.
- **WHY IT WAS FAILING (>50% didn't open):** `Start-Process` only reaches qi's screen from the **MAIN interactive logged-in session**. A Workflow/subagent ship step has NO desktop -> its `Start-Process` opens nothing. **RULE: steps 6-7 MUST run from the MAIN session, never a workflow subagent.** A workflow may do steps 1-5 and RETURN the live URLs; the MAIN session then runs 6-7 on those returned URLs after the workflow completes. Never consider a workflow ship "done" until main has run open + deliver.

### 7. Auto-deliver the live link(s) to qi's Beeper feed -- the leg that never existed
Send every confirmed-live URL to qi's **"Hittl.ink"** WhatsApp self-feed:
- chatID: `!h50wuGNbnpuvJhq2wjOf:beeper.local` (WhatsApp self-chat "Hittl.ink", pinned).
- Tool: `mcp__beeper__send_message`. Batch a cycle's URLs into ONE message (no spam), plain list.
- qi's own self-chat -> always authorized, no recipient risk. It's his running ship-feed so he sees every deliverable on his phone without asking.

### 8. Surface (after 6 + 7)
Emit each live URL as one `Live: https://hitthe.link/<slug>/` line. No "shipping next"/"preview" -- the URL IS the deliverable, already open + already in his feed.

## Per-completion rule
Multi-artifact runs (a BPGS shipping 6 pages): each artifact runs steps 1-5 the instant its file lands; steps 6-7 run from MAIN (consolidated to ONE Beeper message + open-all per ship cycle, but every URL included).

## Quick reference
| Step | Action | Blocks? | Where |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 Stage | `git add <specific>` | no | any |
| 2 Commit | plain msg, no attrib | no | any |
| 3 Rebase | autostash pull --rebase | no | any |
| 4 Push | push (retry 3x) | no | any |
| 5 Poll live | 200 + >500B + no `{{` | YES gate | any |
| 6 Auto-open | Start-Process every URL | no | MAIN interactive only |
| 7 Beeper-deliver | send URLs to Hittl.ink | no | MAIN interactive only |
| 8 Surface | `Live:` lines | after 6+7 | main |

Related: feedback-no-placeholders-ever - feedback-no-commit-credit - feedback-always-check-odd-grid - canon-autoship-floor-48-day - scope-watch-claude-needs-console (why subagents can't auto-open).

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

**USER:**

Base directory for this skill: E:\.claude\skills\omni-comms

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

## agent-a68398b39d85105fd
project: subagents | last active: 2026-06-29 20:04 | 108KB

**USER:**

On Nitro (Windows E: drive), get the full command line and most recent session transcript snippet for these 5 claude.exe PIDs: 92112, 111508, 125848, 132288, 133600.

Use PowerShell:
$pids = @(92112, 111508, 125848, 132288, 133600)
foreach ($p in $pids) {
    $proc = Get-WmiObject Win32_Process -Filter "ProcessId=$p" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($proc) { Write-Host "$p | $($proc.CommandLine.Substring(0,[Math]::Min(200,$proc.CommandLine.Length)))" }

The install itself keeps creating a new temp dir and then failing to delete its own `.git` dir — this is an inherent Windows NTFS git object-file locking bug in the Claude Code plugin installer. The temp dir gets created fresh each install attempt. The root fix is to pre-grant full-control to the `selfexec` user on the entire plugins directory so the process can delete its own temp files:

**ASSISTANT:**

The installer consistently creates a temp dir, clones the plugin repo into it (including a `.git` dir), then fails when trying to `rm` the `.git` dir — because Node.js's `fs.rm` on Windows can't delete `.git` dirs with read-only object files inside them. The icacls grant didn't fully resolve it. Let me check what's actually in that temp dir before it gets cleaned:

**ASSISTANT:**

The issue is structural — the Claude Code plugin installer uses Node.js `fs.rm()` which doesn't handle Windows git's read-only objects in `.git/objects/`. The real fix is to use `git config --global core.autocrlf` or pre-configure git to not set read-only — but more directly, the solution is to set the `GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1` and configure `core.fileMode=false`, or use a workaround: set `GIT_CLONE_DEPTH` or point to a temp dir on a drive with different ACL defaults.

## agent-aadce2a0d2b44db24
project: subagents | last active: 2026-06-29 19:38 | 56KB

**USER:**

On Nitro (Windows E: drive), find the top-level parent Claude Code sessions — the interactive sessions that were opened by the user directly (not spawned as subagents by other sessions).

Top-level sessions are claude.exe processes that:
- Do NOT have --print or -p flag (those are headless subagents)
- Are children of a terminal process (powershell, pwsh, WindowsTerminal) rather than another claude.exe
- Have the largest memory footprint (parent sessions are heavier)

Run this PowerShell to find parent sessions:
Get-WmiObject Win32_Process | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq "claude.exe" } | ForEach-Object {
    $cmd = $_.CommandLine
    $parentProc = Get-WmiObject Win32_Process -Filter "ProcessId=$($_.ParentProcessId)" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    $isTopLevel = $parentProc -and $parentProc.Name -notlike "*claude*"
    $isInteractive = $cmd -notmatch '\-\-print|\s\-p\s|--output-format'
    if ($isTopLevel -or $isInteractive) {
        Write-Host ($_.ProcessId.ToString() + " | parent=" + $parentProc.Name + " | " + $cmd.Substring(0,[Math]::Min(100,$cmd.Length)))
    }
}

Report the PIDs and what each top-level session is doing (from its command line). These are the sessions the user opened himself.

**ASSISTANT:**

| **125848** | pwsh.exe | `E:\.local\bin\claude.exe --teammate-mode tmux --dangerously-skip-permissions --resume` |

**ASSISTANT:**

I'll start by invoking the relevant skill for this comms sweep.

**USER:**

Base directory for this skill: E:\.claude\skills\omni-comms

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

**ASSISTANT:**

I'll load the Beeper tools and read the scope registry in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

Let me read all the active-scope threads in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

Three active threads (DCZ-LaTina, DCZ-Andre, L7S-Luckie) all show our message as most recent — no new unanswered inbound. Now let me locate ELI, MB, PADRE, and TIBA threads.

**ASSISTANT:**

Numeric chatIDs are usable directly. Let me read ELI (most recent active is 458, 1 unread), MB (473), PADRE (35), and TIBA (40).

**ASSISTANT:**

Sweep complete. Every active thread either has our message as the most recent or the only pending inbound is stale/a closer — so this is a conservative no-send sweep. Here's what I found:

**Reasoning per thread:**
- **DCZ-LaTina** — today's exchange closed cleanly: she asked "how are you?", East replied with the check-in + "sending the update by tonight," she said "Awesome! Thx." That's a terminal acknowledgment; the real next step is qi's promised update (not something I should fabricate). No reply warranted.
- **DCZ-Andre** — our check-in (21:37) is the most recent message; his prior inbounds all answered.
- **L7S-Luckie** — our 6/28 13:06 reply already absorbed his Asana drop; our message is newest.
- **ELI-Anthony** — only pending item is a **14-day-old "Missed voice call"** (6/15), not a content inbound. A cold late reply now would be awkward/off — surfacing instead of auto-replying.
- **MB** — our 6/22 "got the flyer + tour date" is newest; nothing new.
- **PADRE** — our 6/22 follow-up ("how'd those leads shake out?") is newest; ball is in his court.
- **TIBA** — monitor-only; 3-week-old "How are you sir" (6/6), read-only by policy.

```
OMNI-COMMS 2026-06-29T21:45Z  (HOTL · conservative · 0 sends)
ACTIVE   : 6 — all in good standing, our msg newest or only-pending is stale/closer
PERSONAL : 0
STRANGER : 0
NOISE    : 1 — Anthony "Missed voice call" (system notif, 14d old)

```

**Per-scope status:**
- **DCZ** → nothing-new (LaTina's "Awesome! Thx" closes today's thread; **open loop: qi owes the promised "update tonight"**). Andre nothing-new.
- **L7S** → nothing-new (Luckie's Asana drop already answered; our msg newest).
- **ELI** → action: needs-qi — stale 14d "Missed voice call" from Anthony, no fresh text; held back late cold reply.
- **MB** → nothing-new (flyer/tour-date answered 6/22).
- **PADRE** → nothing-new (our "how'd leads shake out" follow-up is newest; awaiting his reply).
- **TIBA** → monitor — 3wk-old "How are you sir", surfaced for qi, no auto-reply.

One flag for qi: the DCZ-LaTina thread has East promising "I'll be sending the update by tonight" (21:39) — that deliverable is still owed and is the only true open action from this sweep.

- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.

Let me read the active scope definitions in the registry to get the canonical chatIDs.

I have canonical chatIDs. Now I'll read the newest messages for each active scope's primary thread in parallel.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The registry is the source of truth and it normalizes chatIDs to Beeper Desktop's numeric IDs (e.g. gvoice `467`), not the long Matrix room IDs the prompt header listed. When two ID formats exist for the same thread, the canonical registry value wins — using the prompt's Matrix IDs risks reading/writing the wrong room. Each scope also has *fragmented* threads (same human, multiple surfaces); the "CANONICAL" thread is the one to act on, the rest are monitor-for-context.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**ASSISTANT:**

Sweep complete. I read the newest messages in every active scope plus the monitor-only thread. Here's what I found — and critically, the most recent activity on three threads (DCZ-LaTina, DCZ-Andre, ELI-Anthony) is **qi's own live "testing my new matrix server" sends** from minutes ago (21:35–21:40), so the last word on those is already ours. Nothing warrants a send.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The decisive HOTL signal is **whose message is newest**, not whether an unread flag exists. On every active thread our `isSender:true` message is the latest, or the only new inbound is a terminal pleasantry ("Awesome! Thx") that carries no question or action. Replying to a thank-you closer is exactly the "double-text" the no-spam rule forbids — silence is the correct move. The lone real unanswered inbound (Tiba "How are you sir") sits on a *muted/monitor-only* scope, so it's surfaced for qi, never auto-answered.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

**No messages sent. All threads already answered or qi-handled.**

```
HOTL SWEEP 2026-06-29T21:4x
DCZ/LaTina (467)  -> nothing-new (latest inbound "Awesome! Thx" = terminal closer to qi's own check-in; $111 balance still open, qi said update tonight) 
DCZ/Andre  (396)  -> nothing-new (our check-in is newest; awaiting nothing — site live, content received)
L7S/Luckie (33)   -> nothing-new (our 6/28 ack of Asana drop is newest; ecosystem build in flight)
ELI/Anthony(381)  -> nothing-new ("Is it complete" already answered 18:56; new-user/rosslyn flow is the open BUILD, not a reply; qi test-sends are newest)
MB        (473)   -> nothing-new (our 6/22 flyer/tour ack is newest)
PADRE     (35)    -> nothing-new (our 6/22 re-engage is newest; no inbound since)
TIBA      (40)    -> monitor / SURFACE: stale "How are you sir" (6/06) unanswered — muted scope, qi's call whether to reply
```

One item for your attention, qi: **Tiba (222church)** has a 3-week-old "How are you sir" with no reply — it's a muted/monitor scope so I left it untouched, but say the word and I'll warm it back up. Everything else is clean and current.
