# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

**ASSISTANT:**

Now let me load the beeper tools and read the canonical scope source plus playbooks in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

The task prompt gives matrix-style chatIDs but the registry uses short numeric IDs. Let me verify the Beeper channel is up and pull the registry's active scopes + playbooks before reading any thread.

**ASSISTANT:**

Beeper is up (Google Voice `+1 470-615-9983` connected). Registry uses short numeric chatIDs which the bridge recognizes. Let me pull the newest messages from each active scope's canonical thread in parallel — reading latest first is the no-double-text gate.

**ASSISTANT:**

Sweep complete. I read the newest messages in every active scope's canonical thread first (the no-double-text gate). Here's what I found:

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- The no-spam rule turns on one field: **`isSender`**. In all six threads the *most recent* message is either ours (`isSender:true`) or a terminal courtesy ("Awesome! Thx"). A new inbound only warrants a reply when it's `isSender:false` **and** sits after our last message **and** carries an actual ask.
- The 21:35–21:40 burst ("It's East… new matrix communication server… 1 / 2 / 3 / Testing") across LaTina, Andre and Anthony are **qi's own manual server-test blasts** from this new host — not client traffic. Piling auto-replies on top of a live test would be exactly the double-text the playbook forbids.
- Anthony's only real ask ("Is it complete") was already answered at 18:56 ("still wiring the new-user/new-user-request flow… I'll confirm the second it's live"). Answered → no re-reply.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

No reply is warranted on any scope this cycle. Nothing sent — conservative HOTL hold.

**One-line status:**
- DCZ/LaTina (467) → nothing-new (latest inbound is a terminal "Awesome! Thx" closing qi's test check-in; no ask)
- DCZ/Andre (396) → nothing-new (our check-in is most recent; still awaiting his media)
- L7S/Luckie (33) → nothing-new (our "got the Asana drop" ack is most recent)
- ELI/Anthony (381) → nothing-new ("Is it complete" already answered 18:56; trailing msgs are qi's test blasts)
- MB (473) → nothing-new (our flyer/tour-date ack is most recent)
- PADRE (35) → nothing-new (our lead follow-up is most recent; awaiting his reply)
- TIBA (40) → monitor — surfacing for qi: unanswered "How are you sir" from 6/6 (muted/monitor-only, no auto-reply)

Nothing requires a send. One item for qi's eyes: the stale TIBA greeting, which stays read-only per its muted status.

## Transports

- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.

## Process

List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.

- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |

For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).

- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry

- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

- Read the scope's playbook file for full context + intent-map before replying.
- If a client confirms a payment or sends content, advance per the playbook (issue receipt / start build / push checklist).
- Money: route payment to the payer per playbook; never tell a client to pay twice.
- Content requests for DCZ go to ANDRE first (artist sends content), not LaTina (payer).
- Any git commit: NO AI attribution (plain `slug: what`).

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`



ARGUMENTS: HOTL sweep across active client scopes (DCZ, L7S, ELI, MB, PADRE) + monitor TIBA

- **L7S/Luckie** — our msg is most recent (answered his Asana drop 13:06). Nothing new.

The numeric chatIDs in the registry/prompt (467, 396, 473, 35, 40, 381) are UI indices, not the matrix chatIDs `list_messages` needs. I'll resolve the remaining scopes (ELI, MB, PADRE, TIBA) by searching.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The registry/prompt numeric IDs (467, 396, 473, 35, 40, 381) are **Beeper Desktop UI indices**, not the matrix `chatID`s `list_messages` needs — but the API accepts both forms and resolves them. The prompt's ELI chat #381 was stale; the live Anthony GV thread is **458**. Always resolve via `search_chats` rather than trusting a hardcoded index.

- **DCZ / LaTina** → nothing-new (last inbound "No problem", 6/26 ack). ⚠️ Open loop: we promised her the referral-adjusted $111 balance total "in a bit" — needs the actual referral credit amount before delivery; won't fabricate. **qi: confirm referral credit so I can send the adjusted total.**
- **DCZ / Andre** → nothing-new (our balance-invoice msg is most recent, 6/26).
- **L7S / Luckie** → nothing-new (our 6/28 13:06 reply already absorbed his Asana drop).
- **ELI / Anthony** → monitor — only a stale "Missed voice call" (6/15), no live text; 2-week-old, not auto-replying. **qi: may want to reach back out.**
- **MB** → nothing-new (our 6/22 reply on flyer/tour is most recent; build deliverable "hit you when live" still owed, no new inbound).
- **PADRE** → nothing-new (our 6/22 follow-up is most recent; his last inbound 6/7).
- **TIBA** (monitor-only) → monitor — "How are you sir" (6/6) still unanswered by design; **qi's call.**

One-line summary:
```
DCZ-LaTina  -> nothing-new (flag: owe referral-adjusted $111 total)
DCZ-Andre   -> nothing-new
L7S-Luckie  -> nothing-new
ELI-Anthony -> monitor (stale missed call 6/15)
MB          -> nothing-new
PADRE       -> nothing-new
TIBA        -> monitor (unanswered "How are you sir" 6/6)
```

## **PRECISE CLAUDE CODE BRIEFING (2026)**

### **1. TEAMMATE MODE (`claude --teammate-mode tmux`)**

**What It Is:**
Teammate mode spawns multiple independent Claude Code sessions that work in parallel within a single orchestrated team. It's the experimental feature for **inter-agent coordination**—teammates message each other directly, share a task list, and self-coordinate, unlike subagents (which only report back to the main agent).

**Mechanical Addressing & Handoff:**
- The lead (main session) spawns teammates with a prompt describing roles/count.
- Each teammate gets a **name assigned by the lead** at spawn time.
- Teammates message each other by **name** directly; messages are auto-delivered.
- **Task list** (`~/.claude/tasks/{team-name}/`) is shared; teammates claim work, mark tasks complete.
- Idle notifications auto-fire; no polling required.
- Handoff happens through **SendMessage** (direct inter-agent) or **task assignment**; teammates read `~/teams/{team-name}/config.json` to discover other members.

**Display Modes:**
- `"in-process"` (default): all teammates in one terminal; navigate with arrow keys + Enter to view any teammate.
- `"auto"` / `"tmux"` / `"iterm2"`: split panes if tmux or iTerm2 is available.

**Quota & Billing:**
- **Each teammate consumes its own full context window and tokens independently.** Scales linearly; 5 teammates = ~5× token cost vs. 1 session.
- The lead's quota is separate from teammates.
- Teammates inherit the lead's **permission mode** at spawn; can't set per-teammate permissions at spawn time.

**Use Cases:**
- Parallel code review (security, perf, test coverage each in own teammate).
- Competing hypotheses (debug team debating root cause).
- Multi-layer changes (frontend, backend, tests as separate teammates).

**Key Limitations:**
- No session resumption with in-process teammates (tmux loses teammates on `/resume`).
- One team per session; teammates cannot spawn nested teams.
- Split panes only in tmux/iTerm2, not VS Code integrated terminal or Windows Terminal.

**Enabled by:**
Setting `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` in `settings.json` or environment.

---

### **2. OPUS MODEL MODES & EFFORT SETTINGS**

**The Models & Their Effort Tiers:**
| Model | Available Effort Levels |
|-------|--------------------------|
| **Fable 5** | `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`, `max` |
| **Opus 4.8, 4.7** | `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`, `max` |
| **Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6** | `low`, `medium`, `high`, `max` |

**Default effort by model:**
- Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6: **`high`**
- Opus 4.7: **`xhigh`**

---

**What "Ultracode" Is (Critical Distinction):**
`ultracode` is **NOT a model mode or effort level.** It's a **Claude Code orchestration setting**:
- Sends `xhigh` effort to the model.
- **Additionally** orchestrates [dynamic workflows](/en/workflows) for substantive tasks—Claude auto-builds multi-step execution plans.
- **Session-only** (does not persist across sessions).
- Set via `/effort ultracode`, `--settings '{"ultracode": true}'`, or Agent SDK control request.
- Not part of `effortLevel` setting or `--effort` flag.

So there is **no "Opus Ultra Code" mode**—there's Opus at `xhigh` effort, and separately the `ultracode` session setting that layers workflow orchestration on top.

---

**Effort vs. Fast Mode:**
- **Effort level** (`low`–`max`, `ultracode`): controls adaptive reasoning depth per step (model decides how much to think).
  - Lower = cheaper & faster for routine tasks.
  - Higher = deeper reasoning & higher cost for complex tasks.
  - `max` = no token budget constraint; prone to overthinking.
- **Fast mode** (`/fast`): same model quality, lower latency, higher cost per token.
  - Opus 4.8: $10/$50 input/output per MTok (vs. standard $3/$15).
  - Opus 4.7: $30/$150 input/output per MTok (vs. standard $3/$15).

You can **combine both**: fast mode + low effort = maximum speed on straightforward tasks.

---

**"Auto" Effort Behavior:**
- `/effort auto` resets to the **model's default effort** for the session.
- **Does NOT dynamically pick effort per turn.** It's a one-time reset to that model's default.
- `ultracode` does NOT enable per-turn dynamic effort. It adds workflow orchestration with fixed `xhigh` reasoning per message.

---

**Token Cost Comparison on Same Model (Opus 4.8 example):**
| Setting | Per-token cost | Use case |
|---------|----------------|----------|
| `low` | Baseline (~$3/$15) | Fast, cheap, routine tasks |
| `high` (default) | ~1.5–2× baseline | Balanced (most coding) |
| `xhigh` | ~2.5–3× baseline | Complex reasoning |
| `max` | Uncapped | Demanding tasks, diminishing returns |
| `ultracode` (xhigh + workflows) | 2.5–3× baseline + extra tokens for plan steps | Substantive multi-step tasks with auto-planning |
| `fast mode` + any effort | 3.3× baseline (Opus 4.8) | When latency > cost |

---

**For Someone Orchestrating Sonnet Swarms—Which Default?**
- **If you're the lead, use `ultracode` on the lead session only.**
  - It adds workflow orchestration that coordinates parallel work.
  - Teammates inherit the lead's effort but NOT the `ultracode` setting (it's session-only).
  - Teammates run at their own chosen effort.
  - Cost: lead gets extra orchestration tokens; teammates run standard effort.
  
- **If pure cost matters & work is well-defined:**
  - Lead: `medium` or `high` + subagents (cheaper, no multi-agent overhead).
  - Don't use agent teams unless work genuinely needs inter-agent debate/collaboration.
  - Agent teams scale token cost 5× for 5 teammates; subagents summarize back (lower cost).

- **If work benefits from parallel exploration (research, review, competing hypotheses):**
  - Lead: `ultracode` (plans the parallel work, coordinates handoff).
  - Teammates: `medium` or `high` (cost-effective execution).
  - Cost tradeoff: pay for orchestration on lead; teammates stay lean.

---

**Key Takeaway:**
- "Opus Ultra Code" doesn't exist. There's **Opus + `xhigh` effort + optional `ultracode` session setting** (which adds workflow orchestration).
- `ultracode` is a Claude Code feature, not a model mode.
- Effort and fast mode are orthogonal controls: effort controls reasoning depth, fast mode controls latency/cost.
- For parallel swarms: `ultracode` on lead, medium/high on teammates = structured orchestration + cost efficiency.

---

Sources: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams.md, https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config.md (sections: Adjust effort level, fast mode), https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fast-mode.md


---

**USER:**

Base directory for this skill: E:\.claude\skills\omni-comms

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.

**Per-scope status:**

- **DCZ / LaTina** → nothing-new *(action: we owe her the referral-credit-adjusted $111 balance total — promised 6/26 "in a bit," now 3 days stale. Needs your call on the credit amount; not guessing a discount.)*
- **DCZ / Andre** → nothing-new (our 6/26 balance-invoice note is last; he's quiet, content all received).
- **L7S / Luckie** → nothing-new (we ack'd his Asana-task drop 6/28 13:06; our msg is last).
- **ELI / Anthony (#381)** → nothing-new (answered his "Is it complete" 11 min before sweep; *open build: finish + confirm the new-user/new-user-request flow on the rosslyn login*).

The no-double-text rule keys on whether a genuinely new inbound is *unanswered and warrants a reply*, not merely on "who spoke last." Auto-firing a reply here would be filler spam; the real obligation (the referral-credit number) is a money decision that routes to you, not an autonomous send.

Anthony's threads also show why fragment-thread sweeping matters: GV splits one person across many chat IDs (#381, #522, #523…). # Nemotron-Free as a Hermes Backend — Verdict

**Bottom line: Yes — qi is right, it's genuinely free, OpenAI-compatible, and the larger Nemotron models are built for exactly this (agentic tool-use + long-context orchestration). It drops into Hermes as a `base_url` + `api_key` + `model` swap. The one real catch is the ~40 RPM rate ceiling, not cost or capability.**

## (a) Is Nemotron-free a good Hermes backend?

Yes, with one caveat. The NVIDIA "NIM" / `build.nvidia.com` catalog exposes NVIDIA's own Nemotron models over a **fully OpenAI-compatible** endpoint at **zero token cost**, no credit card. The current (2026) Nemotron 3 family — Nano/Super/Ultra — is explicitly designed for "agentic AI systems where multiple agents collaborate, reason over long horizons, and route work between specialized models," which is precisely Hermes' job (async memory + Claude-dispatch orchestration). They support **function calling in standard OpenAI format** and **1M-token context windows**. So for an orchestrator that plans, holds long memory, and dispatches to Claude Code, this is a strong free brain. The caveat is throughput: the free tier is rate-limited (~40 req/min), so it suits a single-user orchestrator (qi on Nitro), not a high-fan-out swarm hammering it.

## (b) Single best free Nemotron model + exact config

For Hermes' role (orchestration + tool-calling + long memory, cost = $0), the best pick is **Nemotron 3 Super (120B total / 12B active MoE, 1M context)** — flagship reasoning + tool-calling, but MoE-efficient so latency stays reasonable.

OpenAI-compatible config (drop straight into Hermes' gateway config):

```
base_url = https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1
api_key  = nvapi-...        # from https://build.nvidia.com/settings/api-keys
model    = nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b
```

Alternatives, same `base_url`/`api_key`, just swap the model string:
- **Lower latency / lightest, still agentic + 1M ctx:** `nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning` (30B/3B-active, ~377 tok/s, multimodal) — best if Super feels slow.
- **Max reasoning, frontier-scale (slower):** `nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra` (550B/55B-active, 1M ctx) — for hard planning, not hot-path dispatch.
- **Stable older known-good for tool-calling:** `nvidia/llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1` (optimized for reasoning, RAG, tool-calling) — proven, widely mirrored on OpenRouter as a `:free` model too.

Get the key: sign up at `build.nvidia.com` with email → API Keys → generate `nvapi-...`. Any Nemotron model page has a code panel that pre-fills the OpenAI Python snippet (`base_url="https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1"`).

## (c) Top 3 risks

1. **~40 RPM rate cap (the real ceiling).** It's a *global* per-key limit shared across all model calls — parallel Hermes lanes share the same 40/min budget. NVIDIA staff reference ~40 RPM as the baseline; you can request an upgrade to ~200 RPM via the developer forum, and adding a business email unlocks a free 90-day AI Enterprise trial (+~4,000 credits). For a single orchestrator this is fine; for a fan-out swarm it will throttle. Note: sources mildly conflict on credits — some say 1,000–5,000 starter credits with no expiry, others reference a credit-expiry email; treat credits as effectively renewable/free for dev use but don't assume unlimited production throughput.
2. **Privacy / data-retention ambiguity — do not pipe qi's sensitive memory through it raw.** Reporting conflicts: NVIDIA's stated position is requests are stateless with no prompt/response training and minimal operational logging, but third-party analysis and the Trial Terms flag that free-tier inputs/outputs can be recorded, and NVIDIA itself warns "do not upload confidential or personal data." Given Hermes handles qi's "async memory" (potentially family/medical-adjacent context per canon), scrub or gate sensitive content before it hits the free endpoint.
3. **"Dev/test, not production" + hosted-not-local.** The free catalog is explicitly for development/testing (production officially wants an AI Enterprise license), and it's NVIDIA-cloud-hosted — so it adds a network dependency and is not a local/offline backend. If Hermes is meant to be sovereign/always-up on Nitro, the free API is a cloud crutch; the same Nemotron weights are open and self-hostable via NIM/llama.cpp later if you want to cut the cord.

## (d) One-line recommendation

Point Hermes at `https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1` with an `nvapi-` key and `model=nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b` (fall back to `nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning` if latency bites) — it's free, OpenAI-drop-in, tool-calling + 1M-context agentic, just keep it to single-orchestrator throughput (~40 RPM) and scrub sensitive memory before it leaves the box.

---

Sources:
- [NVIDIA NIM API Pricing 2026: Free Tier, 40 RPM & Real Cost](https://decodethefuture.org/en/nvidia-nim-api-pricing-limits-guide/)
- [llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1 — build.nvidia.com](https://build.nvidia.com/nvidia/llama-3_3-nemotron-super-49b-v1)
- [nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b — build.nvidia.com](https://build.nvidia.com/nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b)

# OpenRouter Free-Model Rotation as a Hermes Backend — Verdict

## (a) Is it a good Hermes backend? — Qualified YES, with one hard caveat

For a FREE, no-CC "async memory + Claude-dispatch orchestration" layer, OpenRouter free-model rotation is the strongest free option available in mid-2026 — **but only if you spend the one-time $10**. Without it you get **50 requests/day total**, which dies in minutes under any real orchestration loop. With the $10 (permanent, never expires) you get **1,000 free requests/day** across 28+ free models, and rotation lets you treat that as a shared pool while dodging per-model stalls.

The hard caveat: **free endpoints route to providers that may train on your prompts/completions by default.** For Hermes specifically — which touches qi's memory and dispatch context — this is a privacy exposure. There is a *separate* account toggle for free models ("allow routing to providers that may train"). You must turn that OFF (or enforce ZDR), which will shrink the available free-model pool but is mandatory given canon (qi's data, life-ops, sensitive memory).

Bottom line: good as a free brain for **non-sensitive** orchestration glue (routing decisions, summarization, tool-call dispatch). It is **NOT** a Claude replacement and **NOT** where qi's private memory bodies should be sent raw unless training is disabled. Canon's #1 LAW still holds — M4/Claude is the brain; this is a cheap operate-layer co-processor, exactly the Hermes role.

## (b) Recommended rotation set (3-5 current `:free` IDs, all tool-use capable)

Prioritized for agentic tool-use + context, verified live against `openrouter.ai/api/v1/models?supported_parameters=tools`:

```
1. qwen/qwen3-coder:free                  # 1M ctx, SOTA free coding/tool-use — primary
2. meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free # 131K ctx, rock-solid generalist tool-caller
3. openai/gpt-oss-120b:free               # 131K ctx, strong reasoning + tools
4. qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free  # 262K ctx, fast MoE, good fallback
5. google/gemma-4-31b-it:free             # 262K ctx, multimodal + tools, diversity slot
```

All five report `tools: ✓`. Put `qwen3-coder:free` first (best agentic + 1M window), Llama-3.3-70B second as the reliability anchor, then the rest as failover. Avoid the tiny ones (`nemotron-nano-9b`, `lfm-2.5-1.2b`) for orchestration — too weak for multi-step tool reasoning. Note the catalog churns monthly (the bare `qwen3-coder:free` endpoint has been flagged as at-risk of removal) — Hermes should re-pull the models list periodically rather than hardcoding forever.

## (c) Exact configuration

**Base URL / auth (OpenAI-compatible):**
```
base_url: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
header:   Authorization: Bearer <OPENROUTER_API_KEY>
optional: HTTP-Referer / X-Title  (app attribution for rankings)
```

**Server-side fallback in ONE request — the `models` array.** Pass models in priority order; if the first errors/rate-limits, OpenRouter auto-tries the next. You are billed (here, $0) for whichever model actually answered, returned in the response `model` field:

Raw JSON body:
```json
{
  "models": [
    "qwen/qwen3-coder:free",
    "meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free",
    "openai/gpt-oss-120b:free",
    "qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free",
    "google/gemma-4-31b-it:free"
  ],
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [...]
}
```

OpenAI SDK (the `models` array goes in `extra_body`, NOT `model`):
```python
client = OpenAI(base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", api_key=KEY)
resp = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="qwen/qwen3-coder:free",   # primary
    extra_body={"models": [
        "meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free",
        "openai/gpt-oss-120b:free",
        "qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free",
        "google/gemma-4-31b-it:free",
    ]},
    messages=[...], tools=[...],
)
```

Two extra notes:
- **`models` array handles failover (one model erroring → next).** It does NOT actively load-balance to dodge the 20 rpm cap. To spread load and reduce 429s, Hermes should *also* round-robin the primary `model` client-side each request, then keep the array as the safety net. Best practice = client-side round-robin (spread the 20 rpm / 1000 rpd budget) + server-side `models` fallback (survive a dead provider).
- Alternative: `model: "openrouter/auto"` (Auto Router, NotDiamond-powered) picks per-prompt and pins the model+provider for the rest of a conversation — convenient but it can select **paid** models, so for strictly-free you must stick to the explicit `:free` array, not auto.
- Check budget anytime: `GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/key` returns `limit_remaining` and daily/weekly usage.

## (d) Top risks

1. **Privacy/training (highest).** Free providers may train on prompts by default. Must disable the free-model training toggle / enforce ZDR before Hermes sends any of qi's memory. Otherwise this violates canon on sensitive data.
2. **Throughput ceiling.** Even with $10 → 20 rpm and 1,000 req/day account-wide (shared across ALL free models — rotation does NOT multiply the daily cap; it only smooths per-minute/per-provider stalls). A busy "always-execute, never idle" orchestrator can exhaust 1,000/day. Budget accordingly; fall back to a cheap paid model (e.g. Qwen3-Coder-Plus ~$0.004/req) when the free pool is dry.
3. **Catalog churn / deprecation.** Free model IDs appear and vanish monthly (the `:free` endpoint concept itself has been getting pruned). Hardcoded IDs will silently 404. Hermes must re-discover the list from the models endpoint and self-heal the rotation set.
4. **No uptime/latency SLA.** Free endpoints are best-effort, can be paused or rate-limited by the upstream provider with no warning — fine for async, risky for anything user-facing/voice-path latency-sensitive.
5. **429/Cloudflare throttling.** Bursts beyond reasonable usage get blocked at the edge; needs exponential-backoff + the rotation/round-robin to stay under the line.

## (e) One-line recommendation

Use OpenRouter free-model rotation for Hermes as a $10-once free operate-layer — primary `qwen/qwen3-coder:free` with a 4-model `:free` fallback array, client-side round-robin, training toggle OFF/ZDR enforced, and a paid-model fallback for overflow — but keep qi's sensitive memory and the real "brain" on Claude/M4 per canon.

Sources:
- [OpenRouter Model Fallbacks docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/model-fallbacks)
- [OpenRouter Auto Router docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/routers/auto-router)
- [OpenRouter API Rate Limits docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs/api/reference/limits)
- [OpenRouter live models API (?supported_parameters=tools)](https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models?supported_parameters=tools)
- [OpenRouter Provider Logging / privacy docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/privacy/provider-logging)
- [OpenRouter Data Collection docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/privacy/data-collection)
- [OpenRouter Free Tier 2026 (Klymentiev)](https://klymentiev.com/blog/openrouter-free-tier)
- [OpenRouter Free Models list (CostGoat, Jun 2026)](https://costgoat.com/pricing/openrouter-free-models)

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

**ASSISTANT:**

Now let me load the Beeper tools and read the canonical scope registry in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

I have the tools and the registry confirms the scopes. Let me read the newest messages on each active thread in parallel. I'll start with the ones I have direct chatIDs for.

**ASSISTANT:**

Three threads analyzed — DCZ LaTina, DCZ Andre, and L7S all show OUR message as most recent. Now let me pull ELI, MB, and PADRE. Let me find their chatIDs.

**ASSISTANT:**

ELI and the active Anthony thread — let me read the most-recent Anthony GV chat (458, has unread) and TIBA (monitor-only, chat 40).

**ASSISTANT:**

Sweep complete. Every active thread's newest message read; **no sends warranted** — our message is already the most recent on every workflow thread, so firing anything would be a double-text. One real open action surfaced for qi.

```
OMNI-COMMS 2026-06-29 — HOTL sweep
```

- **DCZ / LaTina** → nothing-new. Last inbound "No problem" (6/26) is an ack to our promise. ⚠️ **action owed:** we told her we'd send the referral-credit-adjusted $111 balance total "in a bit" (msg 34632) — never sent. Needs qi's referral-credit amount before I quote a number; not auto-sending a guess.
- **DCZ / Andre** → nothing-new. Our balance-invoice msg (6/26) is latest; awaiting his media tweaks / payment.
- **L7S / Luckie** → nothing-new. We already answered his latest Asana drop (6/28 13:06). Building L7SINC.COM ecosystem; no inbound since.
- **ELI / Anthony** → monitor. Newest is a 2-week-old "Missed voice call" notice (6/15, still unread) — no text content to answer; a cold "sorry I missed you" 2 wks late reads wrong. Flagging, not auto-replying. (Note: prompt's #381 not found; 458 is the live thread.)
- **MB** → nothing-new. Our "adding flyer + tour date to bookmb.com" (6/22) is latest.
- **PADRE** → nothing-new. Our re-engage nudge (6/22) is latest; no reply from him.
- **TIBA (monitor-only)** → surface: "How are you sir" (6/6, unread). Per scope, read-only — not replying. Nothing urgent for qi.

Net: **0 replied, 0 sent** (correct under no-double-text), 1 PERSONAL/monitor flag (Anthony missed call), 1 monitor (Tiba). **Only action requiring qi:** the DCZ referral-credit number so I can send LaTina her adjusted balance.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

**ASSISTANT:**

I'll run the HOTL sweep. Let me load the beeper tools and read the scope registry plus playbooks first.

**ASSISTANT:**

Now let me read the latest messages from each active scope thread in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

Three active threads read. DCZ-LaTina, DCZ-Andre, and L7S all have our message as the most recent — nothing new to answer. Now let me locate the remaining scopes (Anthony/ELI, MB, Padre, Tiba).

**ASSISTANT:**

Found the threads. The canonical ELI thread is pinned chatID 381 (latest activity today 15:19). Let me read it plus MB (359), Padre (35), Tiba (40).

**ASSISTANT:**

Sweep complete. Every active thread already has our message as the most recent — **no new unanswered inbound exists on any client scope**, so the no-double-text rule means no sends. Details:

```
OMNI-COMMS  2026-06-29T15:25Z  (HOTL sweep — 0 sends)
ACTIVE   : 6 — all already answered; our msg is newest on each
PERSONAL : 0
STRANGER : 0
MONITOR  : 1 — Tiba (unanswered greeting, monitor-only → surfaced)
NOISE    : 0
```

**Per-scope status:**

- **DCZ / LaTina** → nothing-new. Last inbound "No problem" (6/26 23:35) is a closer to our balance-invoice + referral-credit note; no question pending. ⚠️ Open promise on *our* side: we said we'd send her the "updated total" adjusted for the referral credit — that's a pending action (needs the actual credit amount decided), not a reply. Not sending to avoid double-text.
- **DCZ / Andre** → nothing-new. Our balance-invoice note (6/26 20:48) is newest; no inbound since.
- **L7S / Luckie** → nothing-new. We answered his Asana drop at 6/28 13:06; nothing newer from him.
- **ELI / Anthony (381)** → nothing-new. We replied to his "Gm" at 13:07 today; build work in flight (new-user flow + rosslyn login). Note: a stray **"Testing"** went out from our line at 15:19 today — harmless but flagging it as not-client-intended.
- **MB (359)** → nothing-new. Our last msg moved him to the main line (6/18); no inbound since. (Old un-closed loop: his 6/15 content360-app question got "I'll look into it now" then a line-switch — surfacing in case qi wants to answer it.)
- **PADRE (35)** → nothing-new. Our re-engagement nudge (6/22) is newest; no reply from Padre.

**Monitor surface (no reply sent):**
- **TIBA (40)** → Tiba sent **"How are you sir"** on **2026-06-06** (still unread, ~3 weeks). Monitor-only scope per rules, so not auto-replying — **flagging for qi**: a warm prospect greeting is sitting unanswered. Your call whether to re-open.

No git commit, no TTS (triage is text-only, nothing shipped). Net: clean inbox across all active scopes; two items for your decision (LaTina's referral-credit total; whether to revive Tiba).

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The whole sweep hinges on one cheap signal: the newest message's `isSender` flag. If `true`, we've already answered — replying would double-text. `─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Base directory for this skill: E:\.claude\skills\omni-comms

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

**ASSISTANT:**

I'll run the HOTL sweep across the active scopes. Let me load the Beeper tools and read each thread's latest messages.

**ASSISTANT:**

I'll read each active scope's latest messages. Let me pull them in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

Three scopes read — all have our message as the most recent (nothing new). Now let me locate ELI (Anthony), MB, PADRE, and TIBA threads.

**ASSISTANT:**

Anthony's canonical pinned thread is chatID 381 (active today). Let me read it plus MB (473), PADRE (35), and TIBA (40).

**ASSISTANT:**

Sweep complete. Every active thread already has our message as the most recent — **no new unanswered inbound on any scope**, so per the no-double-text rule I'm sending nothing. One open loop worth surfacing.

```
OMNI-COMMS 2026-06-29T15:2X
ACTIVE   : 6 — all answered; no new inbound to reply to
PERSONAL : 0
MONITOR  : 1 — TIBA pending (qi decision)
NOISE    : 0
```

**Per-scope status:**

- **DCZ / LaTina** → nothing-new. Last inbound "No problem" (6/26) was an ack of our promise to send the referral-adjusted total. ⚠️ **action (qi):** we still owe LaTina the updated balance number — needs the referral-credit amount from you before I send. No auto-send (money/decision).
- **DCZ / Andre** → nothing-new. Our balance-invoice nudge (6/26) is the latest; awaiting his tweaks/payment.
- **L7S / Luckie** → nothing-new. Acked his Asana drop (6/28 13:06); build continues, no reply owed.
- **ELI / Anthony (381)** → nothing-new. His "Gm" answered 13:07; new-user/rosslyn flow is build-side, not comms. (Note: a stray "Testing" went out on this thread at 15:19 from the line — flagging in case that wasn't intended.)
- **MB (473)** → nothing-new. Flyer/tour-date confirmed 6/22; awaiting next drop.
- **PADRE (35)** → nothing-new. Our lead-shakeout check-in (6/22) stands; awaiting his reply.
- **TIBA (40)** → monitor. Unanswered "How are you sir" (6/6) sitting unread — surfacing only, no auto-reply per monitor rule. Your call if you want a warm touch.

Net: zero sends (conservative HOTL — no genuine new inbound). One decision pending from you: the DCZ referral-credit figure so I can close LaTina's balance total.

**USER:**

Base directory for this skill: E:\.claude\skills\omni-comms

# omni-comms — Beeper omni-inbox read · triage · HOTL reply

Pull every inbound across the omni-inbox, triage it, and prepare replies with a human on the loop. Two hard rails: (1) never leave a real workflow thread on read, (2) never blanket auto-reply to strangers. Draft for known/active threads; surface unknowns for qi.

## When to fire

Invoke when qi:
- Asks "omni-comms" / "check my inbox" / "triage Beeper" / "what came in" / "any new texts" / "who needs a reply"
- Asks to "draft replies" / "sweep the inbox"
- An inbound lands on an active workflow thread (Anthony, the 4 loop accounts, etc.) — surface it, don't sit on it

## Transports

Read/send via the Beeper Desktop API + selmcp:
- Desktop API on :23373 (proxy :23374). selmcp exposes `beeper__*` tools (list chats, read thread, send).
- If :23373 refuses or `beeper__*` 404s, the whole bee channel is down — the fix is GUI-only (Beeper Settings > Desktop API ON). Surface that and STOP; do not attempt headless toggling.
- SMS real path is the Google Voice bridge into Beeper; qi's agent line is +1 770-765-2217 (GV), primary personal is +1 404-734-8672. Do not cross them.

ALWAYS verify a send landed by re-reading the thread. A `send_message` "upstream timeout" STILL delivers — never blind-retry; read the thread first.

## Process

### 1 — Pull inbound
List recent chats with unread/inbound activity across all channels (SMS/GV, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.). Pull the last N messages per active thread for context.

### 2 — Triage each thread into a lane
- **ACTIVE WORKFLOW** — known counterpart on a live thread (Anthony Vasquez, the 4 loop accounts: l7s / tracking-together / padre / sean, current client work). Standing policy: never leave on read, consistent East voice, no leak across threads.
- **KNOWN PERSONAL** — qi's real contacts; surface + offer a draft, but qi sends.
- **STRANGER / COLD** — unknown sender, spam, cold outreach. NEVER auto-reply. Surface a one-line summary; qi decides if it even warrants a response.
- **NOISE** — automated/notification/marketing. Summarize count, take no action.

### 3 — Reply policy by lane
| Lane | Action | Auto-send? |
|------|--------|-----------|
| Active workflow | Draft in East voice, respecting that thread's mode | Per that thread's policy — loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default; auto-send ONLY where the account-config explicitly allows |
| Known personal | Draft, surface to qi | No — qi sends |
| Stranger / cold | Summarize only | Never |
| Noise | Count + ignore | Never |

Anthony's requests: auto-BPGS them (turn the ask into proposed parallel work), keep East voice, never leak — but the reply itself stays HOTL unless his thread's config says otherwise.

### 4 — Surface the sweep
Emit one compact triage block (text only — comms triage is not voiced):
```
OMNI-COMMS <ISO timestamp>
ACTIVE   : <n> — <thread: one-line + draft status>
PERSONAL : <n> — <thread: one-line, draft ready>
STRANGER : <n> — <sender: one-line, NO auto-reply>
NOISE    : <n> — ignored
```
For each ACTIVE/PERSONAL thread show the proposed reply text so qi can approve or one-tap send.

### 5 — Send + verify (only where authorized)
For threads cleared to send (active-workflow with auto-send policy, or qi-approved): send via `beeper__*`, then RE-READ the thread to confirm delivery. Log the send. Never blind-retry on timeout.

## Banned actions
- No blanket auto-reply to strangers — ever.
- No leaving an active workflow thread on read — surface it immediately.
- No voice leak — keep the East voice consistent; never reveal the agent layer or cross-thread context.
- No blind retry on "upstream timeout" — verify by reading first (the message likely already sent).
- No focus-stealing pings — surface in the sweep; don't fire unsolicited notifications.
- No TTS — comms triage surfaces as text only.

## Related canon
- [[anthony-vasquez-comms]] — never leave on read, auto-BPGS, consistent East voice, no leak (Beeper GV chat 384)
- [[feedback-auto-beeper-always]] — monitor all Beeper; auto-act on active workflow threads, never blanket-reply strangers
- [[feedback-beeper-timeout-still-delivers]] — "upstream timeout" still delivers; verify, never blind-retry
- [[account-configs-4]] — the 4 loop accounts; loop is DRAFT-only/HOTL & DISABLED by default
- [[beeper-desktop-api-access]] — :23373/:23374, the API-OFF whole-channel failure + GUI fix
- [[phone-registry]] — agent line 770-765-2217 vs primary personal 404-734-8672 (don't cross)
- [[feedback-no-focus-stealing]] — no unsolicited pings/notifications

## Completion narration (VVS — Commandment 15)
On completion, ALWAYS narrate the result aloud so qi hears it. Execution/completion narration is ON (per vvs-voice-scope); only BPGS *proposals* stay muted.
- On the Mac:  `xen-exodus-narrate "<this skill> complete: <one-line result>"`
- From Windows/elsewhere:  `ssh qi@100.97.145.50 'zsh -lc "xen-exodus-narrate \"<this skill> complete: <one-line result>\""'`

**ASSISTANT:**

I'll run the HOTL sweep. Let me load the Beeper tools and read the scope registry + playbooks in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

Beeper tools loaded. Let me verify the channel is up and pull the active threads in parallel.

**ASSISTANT:**

Channel is UP. **DCZ-Andre** latest is our message (nothing-new). **L7S-Luckie** latest is our 6/28 reply (nothing-new). **DCZ-LaTina** latest inbound is "No problem" (6/26) — but we have an open promise to send her an adjusted total with referral credit. Let me read the DCZ playbook and locate the remaining scope threads.

**ASSISTANT:**

MB = chat **359**. Anthony (ELI) latest is pinned chat **381** (active today 6/29). Let me read those plus PADRE (35) and TIBA (40).

**ASSISTANT:**

Sweep complete. Channel is UP, all 7 threads read newest-first. **No sends warranted** — every active thread's latest is either already answered by us or needs qi's input. Conservative HOTL held; zero double-texts.

```
OMNI-COMMS 2026-06-29T15:25Z  (HOTL sweep · 0 sent)
ACTIVE   : 5
  DCZ/LaTina (#467) → action: we owe the promised "updated total" — 6/26 we said we'd
     apply the referral credit to her $111 balance & send the adjusted number; her "No problem"
     was just an ack. Credit $ amount is qi's call (playbook: don't auto-bill) → NOT fabricating.
     NEEDS qi: the referral-credit figure, then I send the revised balance.
  DCZ/Andre (#396) → nothing-new (our 6/26 balance-invoice note is latest; awaiting payment)
  L7S/Luckie (WA) → nothing-new (our 6/28 13:06 "got the Asana drop" is latest; build in flight)
  ELI/Anthony (#381) → nothing-new (his "Gm" answered 6/29; new-user flow on Tracking Together
     being built w/ rosslyn login — open build, no pending inbound)
  PADRE (#35) → nothing-new (our 6/22 "how'd the leads shake out" nudge is latest)
PERSONAL : 0
MONITOR  : 2
  TIBA (#40) → SURFACE to qi: Tiba's "How are you sir" (6/06) still unread/unanswered.
     Monitor-only scope — not auto-replying. qi's call if/how to respond (stale 3wk).
  MB (#359) → nothing-new (we migrated MB to the main 9983 line 6/18; this GV thread is dormant.
     Stale unanswered "is content360 app any good?" from 6/15 — flag only, thread deprecated)
STRANGER : 0
NOISE    : 0
```

**Two things for qi:**
1. **DCZ/LaTina** — one open promise to close: the referral-credit-adjusted $111 balance total. Give me the credit amount and I'll send it (won't invent a figure per the no-nickel-and-diming / verify rules).
2. **TIBA** — friendly check-in sitting unread since 6/6; monitor-only so I left it for you.

Note: Anthony's GV thread is split across many duplicate rooms (#521/#522 show "1 unread") — I treated pinned **#381** (live, active today) as canonical and did **not** chase the duplicates to avoid cross-thread double-texting. Flag if you want those reconciled.
